mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and data sources within Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer to manage their resources using GitOps workflows or infrastructure as code. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes knowledge and the potential complexity of managing multiple Grafana instances.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Grafana Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator using Helm, specifying the version to ensure compatibility.

```bash

helm install grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2 --namespace grafana-operator --create-namespace

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator in a new namespace.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a Grafana instance using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> Creates a Grafana instance with specified configurations.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana -n grafana-operator

```

> Lists the Grafana instances in the specified namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Grafana instances are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator --namespace grafana-operator

kubectl delete namespace grafana-operator

```

> Uninstalls the Grafana Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana instances. This step will upgrade the Grafana Operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get grafana -n grafana-operator -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2 --namespace grafana-operator

kubectl get grafana -n grafana-operator

```

> Backs up the current Grafana instance configuration.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator.
> Verifies the Grafana instance status after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana -n grafana-operator -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Grafana" in version "grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Grafana CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Grafana Operator is installed correctly and the CRDs are applied.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install Grafana: Error: release grafana-operator failed: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Helm chart version is incompatible with the Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Check the compatibility of the Helm chart version with your Kubernetes version and adjust accordingly.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.30

troubleshooting.title: Grafana instance is not starting.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to misconfiguration in the Grafana CR.

**Fix:**
Review the Grafana CR configuration for errors and apply the correct settings.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get grafana -n grafana-operator returns no resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that no Grafana instances have been created.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Grafana CR was applied successfully and check for any errors in the operator logs.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD handling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance listed with the command 'kubectl get grafana -n grafana-operator', showing its status as 'Running'.

    security:

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and tools installed.
